title: Unraveling Time's Fabric: A Metaphysical Journey into Time Travel

questions.sentence: Beyond the thrilling narratives of science fiction, the concept of time travel plunges us into a profound philosophical labyrinth, challenging our most fundamental assumptions about reality, causality, and identity.

To reconcile this, some theorists propose the Novikov Self-Consistency Principle, which posits that any attempt to alter the past would inevitably fail or lead to an outcome consistent with the established timeline.
Imagine a time traveler bringing a valuable scientific discovery, say Einstein's theory of relativity, back to a past era before Einstein himself formulated it.

In stark contrast, ‘Eternalism’ (often associated with the ‘Block Universe’ theory) posits that all moments in time – past, present, and future – exist simultaneously and are equally real, forming a static four-dimensional spacetime block.

If a time traveler were to go back and prevent their grandfather's meeting, they would simply create a new, divergent timeline where they never existed, while their original timeline remains unchanged.
